SEARCH
Package com.mps.deepviolet.api

Interface IX509Certificate

public interface IX509Certificate
DeepViolet X.509 certificate representation. Most values come from an java.security.cert.X509Certificate instance upon initialization. However, some values are computed for convenience like CERT_FINGERPRINT which represents the certificate fingerprint computed with the signing algorithm.
Author:
Milton Smith

  • Method Details

    • getSigningAlgorithm

      String getSigningAlgorithm()
      Certificate signing algorithm
      Returns:
      Signing algorithm

    • getSigningAlgorithmOID

      String getSigningAlgorithmOID()
      OID of certificate signing algorithm
      Returns:
      OID in String form

    • getCertificateSerialNumber

      BigInteger getCertificateSerialNumber()
      Certificate serial number
      Returns:
      Certificate serial number

    • getCertificateVersion

      int getCertificateVersion()
      Certificate version number
      Returns:
      Certificate version number

    • getNotValidBefore

      String getNotValidBefore()
      Certificate not valid before timestamp
      Returns:
      Timestamp certificate not valid before.

    • getNotValidAfter

      String getNotValidAfter()
      Certificate not valid after timestamp
      Returns:
      Timestamp certificate not valid after.

    • getValidityState

      IX509Certificate.ValidState getValidityState()
      Certificate validity state.
      Returns:
      Validity state. Supported states, IX509Certificate.ValidState.EXPIRED, certificate is expired. The certificate was once valid but no longer valid. IX509Certificate.ValidState.NOT_YET_VALID, the certificate is not ready for use. IX509Certificate.ValidState.VALID, certifidate ready for deployment and operations.

    • getSubjectDN

      String getSubjectDN()
      Certificate Distinguished Name. Includes information that uniquely identifies the subject. Usually the subject is the host but could be other things in the future like code signer, etc.
      Returns:
      DN of the subject.

    • getIssuerDN

      String getIssuerDN()
      Distinguished Name of the issuer. Includes information that uniquely identifies the issuing authority a Certificate Authority.
      Returns:
      DN of the issuer.

    • getCertificateFingerPrint

      String getCertificateFingerPrint()
      Digital fingerprint of the certificate using the target algorithm identified by the certificate. For example, an MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256 hash.
      Returns:
      String representation of an octet sequence.

    • getTrustState

      IX509Certificate.TrustState getTrustState()
      Trust state of the certificte.
      Returns:
      Supported states, IX509Certificate.TrustState.UNTRUSTED, certificate is not be trusted. IX509Certificate.TrustState.UNKNOWN, the trust state of the certificate cannot be determined. For example, if a certificate is being examined and the owning host cannot be determined or contacted. IX509Certificate.TrustState.TRUSTED, certificate has past trust certification process.

    • isSelfSignedCertificate

      boolean isSelfSignedCertificate()
      The signing authority is also the subject.
      Returns:
      true, if the subjectDN of the certificate equals the issuerDN.

    • isJavaRootCertificate

      boolean isJavaRootCertificate()
      The certificate traces back to active Java trust store. Usually, the default trust store and root Certificate Authorities that ship with Java. Note: roots change between different versions of Java. Oracle removes, adds, and updates roots regularly.
      Returns:
      true, the current certificate is a root in the active trust store. false, the current certificate is not a root in the active trust store.

    • getNonCritOIDProperties

      String[] getNonCritOIDProperties()
      Non-critical OIDs.
      Returns:
      Property names of non-critical OIDs.

    • getNonCritPropertyValue

      String getNonCritPropertyValue(String key)
      Non-critical OID values
      Parameters:
      key - Key name of key/value pair.
      Returns:
      Property values for non-critical OIDs

    • isContainsNonCritPropertyKey

      boolean isContainsNonCritPropertyKey(String key)
      Test for the presence of non-critical OID key names
      Parameters:
      key - Key name of key/value pair.
      Returns:
      true, key exists. false, key does not exist.

    • getCritOIDProperties

      String[] getCritOIDProperties()
      Critical OIDs.
      Returns:
      Property names of critical OIDs.

    • getCritPropertyValue

      String getCritPropertyValue(String key)
      Critical OID values
      Parameters:
      key - Key name of key/value pair.
      Returns:
      Property values for critical OIDs

    • isContainsCritPropertyKey

      boolean isContainsCritPropertyKey(String key)
      Test for the presence of Critical OID key names
      Parameters:
      key - Key name of key/value pair.
      Returns:
      true, key exists. false, key does not exist.

    • getCertificateChain

      IX509Certificate[] getCertificateChain() throws DeepVioletException
      Certificate chain of trust for the current certificate.
      Returns:
      Array of certificates chaining back to a root. Note: the certificates and root may or may not be trusted.
      Throws:
      DeepVioletException - Thrown on problems.

    • getPublicKeyAlgorithm

      String getPublicKeyAlgorithm()
      Public key algorithm (RSA, EC, DSA, etc.)
      Returns:
      Public key algorithm name

    • getPublicKeySize

      int getPublicKeySize()
      Public key size in bits
      Returns:
      Key size in bits

    • getPublicKeyCurve

      String getPublicKeyCurve()
      For EC keys, the curve name (e.g., secp256r1). Null for non-EC keys.
      Returns:
      Curve name or null

    • getDaysUntilExpiration

      long getDaysUntilExpiration()
      Days until certificate expiration. Negative if expired.
      Returns:
      Days remaining

    • getSubjectAlternativeNames

      List<String> getSubjectAlternativeNames()
      Subject Alternative Names from the certificate.
      Returns:
      List of SANs (DNS names, IPs, emails, URIs). Empty if no SAN extension.

    • getRevocationStatus

      IRevocationStatus getRevocationStatus()
      Get revocation status for this certificate.
      Returns:
      Revocation status or null if not checked.

    • toString

      String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • equals

      boolean equals(Object obj)
      Test for equality. Two objects are considered equal if, 1) obj is an instance of IX509Certificate, 2) all key/value pairs are equal, 3) the length of obj.getPropertyNames().length()==this.getPropertyNames().length()
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      obj - Object to compare
      Returns:
      true, obj and this are equal. false, obj and this are not equal
